Viewer Guide Desktop First XR Ready

How to use the viewer

Load a volume, choose a mode, and interact with the slices or mask using the mouse. XR controls are still available, but the browser workflow is now the easiest way to work.

Quick Start

The shortest path from opening the app to editing data.

1

Load data

Use the Volume and Mask folders in the GUI, or load the example files first.

2

Pick a mode

Switch modes with 1 2 3 4 or from the Mode folder in the GUI.

3

Work with the mouse

Use left mouse for tool actions, right drag to orbit the camera, and the wheel to zoom.

Desktop Essentials

These shortcuts work across the browser workflow.

1234

Switch to Place, Inspect, Edit, or Segment.

Right Drag

Orbit the camera around the scene.

Wheel

Zoom the camera in or out.

CtrlZ

Undo in the active mode.

CtrlY

Redo in the active mode.

X

Toggle brush or prompt label between add and subtract.

C

Clear all prompt points in Segment.

G

Reset the current mode state.

Modes

Each mode focuses on one kind of task.

1

Place

Position the volume assembly in the scene.

  • Left drag: move the volume
  • Alt + left drag: rotate the volume
  • Shift + wheel: scale the volume
2

Inspect

Navigate and reposition the slice planes.

  • Left drag on a slice: move the selected plane
  • Alt + left drag: rotate the slice stack
  • Double click: hide or reveal a plane
3

Edit

Paint directly into the mask on the active slice.

  • Left drag: paint mask values
  • Shift + wheel: resize the brush
  • Double click or X: toggle add/subtract
4

Segment

Place prompts and let the model propose a mask.

  • Left click: add a prompt point
  • Alt + left drag: move the slice like Inspect mode
  • Alt + wheel: step through slices with fine control
  • Shift + wheel: resize the prompt brush

Desktop Workflow

The recommended way to use the tool in the browser.

  • Load a sample volume first if you want to learn the interface quickly.
  • Use Inspect to line up the slice you care about before editing or segmenting.
  • Use Edit when you want direct manual control over the mask.
  • Use Segment when placing positive or negative prompt points is faster than painting.

XR Gestures

If you open the tool in AR, the original gesture workflow is still available.

  • Swipe left/right: change mode
  • Swipe down/up: undo or redo
  • Double tap: context action for the current mode
  • Hold / pan / pinch / twist: manipulate the active object for the current mode
